channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: The third Gulf war will scar energy markets for a long time yet
item.description: Residual risks and ruined infrastructure will keep prices high

item.title: Cease-Fire Confusion Over Lebanon
item.description: Disagreements over what was agreed to threaten to unravel the U.S.-Iran cease-fire deal.

item.title: Every company is now a media company—and every boss a star
item.description: The rise of the chatter-industrial complex

item.title: With the ceasefire looking shaky, the region questions its future
item.description: Even as America and Iran pause, the war reveals how exposed the Gulf has become

item.title: With the ceasefire looking shaky, the Gulf questions its future
item.description: Even as America and Iran pause, the war reveals how exposed the region has become

item.title: A New Legal Blow to the U.K.’s Chagos Islands Deal
item.description: Britain’s plan to hand back its last African colony is on thin ice.

item.title: The One Thing That Terrifies Donald Trump
item.description: Iran discovered his Achilles’ heel—a market slump.

item.title: The Energy Crisis Won’t End Right Away (Even if the Iran War Does)
item.description: Even if the cease-fire holds, it will take months to undo the damage.

item.title: Beijing Prefers Peace to Force on Taiwan
item.description: China sees the potential for conflict as a tragedy, not an opportunity.

item.title: 5 Unanswered Questions on the U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire
item.description: The fragile truce faces significant obstacles.

item.title: The war was steadily spiralling in scope and destruction
item.description: America, Israel and Iran were striking a widening array of targets across an expanding area

item.title: How dangerous is Mythos, Anthropic’s new AI model?
item.description: Dario Amodei’s warnings should not be dismissed

item.title: Beijing Is Trying to Break U.S. Narratives Over Taiwan
item.description: A key meeting with the Taiwanese opposition signals a different model of cross-strait stability.

item.title: How Pakistan Helped Secure a Cease-Fire in Iran
item.description: The truce remains fragile, but Islamabad has offered to host peace talks.

item.title: The ceasefire puts Israel in an awkward spot
item.description: Binyamin Netanyahu could yet be transformed from partner to scapegoat

item.title: Partner, scapegoat or spoiler? Israel’s place in a fragile ceasefire
item.description: The dealmaking between America and Iran is happening without Binyamin Netanyahu

item.title: Mummified reptiles are revealing how breathing evolved
item.description: The question had previously been up in the air

item.title: AI models could offer mathematicians a common language
item.description: Some hope they will simplify the process of verifying proofs

item.title: The Fall of Gazprom
item.description: Eastern European energy-sector dynamics have become an accurate signal of broader macroeconomic trends. Through the Polish, Russian, and Hungarian energy conglomerates, one can glimpse three political-economic models at work, only one of which holds any promise for sustained, healthy development.

item.title: Iraq’s Pro-Iran Groups Aren’t Going Anywhere
item.description: Militias aligned with Tehran are embedded in some of the country’s most powerful networks.

item.title: Hegseth’s Divine War
item.description: The U.S. defense secretary is using the military to promote Christian nationalism, experts say.

item.title: Why Trump Mishandled Iran
item.description: The U.S. president has a history of following other world leaders—or his gut—instead of his own intelligence officers and experts.

item.title: Iran Conflict Threatens Armenia-Azerbaijan Progress
item.description: Trump’s Iran policy might undermine his one successful peace accord.

item.title: The IMF’s Spring Meetings Must Deliver Three Reforms
item.description: Kenya’s recent decision to forgo funding from the International Monetary Fund illustrates the asymmetry at the heart of the multilateral financial architecture. As policymakers gather for the Spring Meetings, they have an opportunity to address these structural imbalances, starting with an overhaul of the IMF’s quota system.

item.title: How France learned to fight Russian disinformation
item.description: The EU’s most-targeted country is counterattacking against fake news

item.title: China Is Having a Good War—So Far
item.description: So far, China is weathering the US/Israeli war with Iran better than many of its neighbors, and it is treading cautiously as it seeks opportunities to profit from the fallout. While other major powers commit spectacular strategic blunders, Chinese policymakers are watching and learning.

item.title: The Iran War Is a Boon for the BRICS
item.description: More than a month into the US-Israeli war against Iran, the broader strategic implications are coming into sharper focus. While the two-week ceasefire has provided Donald Trump with a possible off-ramp, China, India, and other powers suddenly find themselves in a much stronger geopolitical position.

item.title: Can Hungary Reverse Course?
item.description: As Hungarians head to the polls, the rest of the world will be watching closely to see if Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's carefully crafted system of free but unfair elections is still functioning as intended. With his challenger commanding a double-digit lead in opinion polls, three scenarios remain possible.

item.title: Is Pakistan’s Peacemaker Role Driven by Ambition or Necessity?
item.description: For Islamabad, mediation is not a display of geopolitical muscle but an act of strategic self-preservation.

item.title: Pakistan as Peacemaker Facilitates the US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: The U.S. appears to have set aside its ambition of pursuing regime change in Iran, as it is now negotiating directly with the system it sought to replace.

item.title: Why China’s Quiet Mediation Could Pave the Way for Easing Pakistan-Afghanistan Tensions
item.description: China’s preferred style of mediation involves quiet and behind-the-scenes facilitation rather than high-profile spectacles or photo-ops.

item.title: The Real Question About the AI Future
item.description: Whereas US power in the 20th century rested heavily on manufacturing scale, military reach, and dollar strength, in the 21st century it may rest increasingly on ownership of indispensable AI infrastructure. The challenge for the world will be how to pay for access to it.

item.title: Iran and America agree to pause their war
item.description: A two-week truce is a start but questions remain about a more enduring deal

item.title: Don't Partition Sudan Again
How the Iran War Will Upend the Global Economy
item.description: The Iran war risks not just an energy Shock—but also a debt crisis.

item.title: America and Israel Have Different Endgames in Iran
item.description: A Shared Enemy, but Diverging Views of Its Motives and Character.

item.title: The crew of Artemis II are returning to a planet they have cheered up
item.description: The mission has recaptured some of the enthusiasm of early lunar flights

item.title: China’s Emergence as a Backroom Mediator
item.description: Reading the tea leaves in South Asia and the Middle East, Beijing is positioning itself as a peacemaker and a stabilizing force in the present geopolitical flux.

item.title: The Philippine’s Strategic Dilemma in the Shadow of Middle East Conflict
item.description: The Iran war is not merely an economic shock for the Philippines, it is a strategic stress test of the Marcos Jr. administration’s pro-U.S. alignment.

item.title: The Roots of China’s Communist Revolution
item.description: Insights from Frank Dikötter

item.title: How the Iran war has sowed panic among farmers
item.description: Soaring prices for fertiliser and fuel could cause a global food shock

item.title: Why East Asia Isn’t Surprised by the Rupture in the Global Order
item.description: East Asia has long balanced the competing dynamics of competition and cooperation, yet the challenge of navigating them has never been greater.

item.title: Welcome to Cryptostan: Kyrgyzstan and the Emerging Crypto Corridor
item.description: Kyrgyzstan has become a de facto “crypto corridor” linking sanctioned Russian flows with trade in Central Asia and supply chains from China.

item.title: Whose Water Powers the Cloud? Data Centers and the Right to Water in Johor
item.description: When Singapore restricted data center growth to protect its own resources, the environmental cost shifted to Johor in neighboring Malaysia.
